provenance.source_corpus: Vegetation Dynamics on an Abandoned Vacuum-Mined Peatland: 5 Years of Monitoring

source_spans.text: The water table level (lower than 40 cm below the soil surface) and frost heaving appear to be the main factors explaining the failure of cotton-grass and of other typical bog plant species to colonize abandoned mined surfaces.

The water table level (lower than 40 cm below the soil surface) and frost heaving appear to be the main factors explaining the failure of cotton-grass and of other typical bog plant species to colonize abandoned mined surfaces.

nodes.name: failure of cotton-grass and of other typical bog plant species to colonize abandoned mined surfaces

source_spans.text: The water table level (lower than 40 cm below the soil surface) and frost heaving appear to be the main factors explaining the failure of cotton-grass and of other typical bog plant species to colonize abandoned mined surfaces.
Frost heaving is the most probable cause of uprooting, and this may explain why so few new tussocks established, although numerous seeds were produced and seedlings germinated each year.
Even a slight increase in the water table level seems to improve growth conditions for many bog species, as observed at the RDLP2 site.
Even a slight increase in the water table level seems to improve growth conditions for many bog species, as observed at the RDLP2 site.
minimal restoration activities, such as blocking drainage ditches, may favour the rapid re-establishment of a dense cotton-grass cover and of many other typical bog plant species

nodes.name: rapid re-establishment of a dense cotton-grass cover
